title: englerina cordata
intro: Englerina cordata is a species of mistletoe, a parasitic plant that grows on the branches of its host tree, primarily found in tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Englerina cordata include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. Traditionally, the plant can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, which are often used to treat various ailments in traditional African medicine.
